author: Dr. Lizard
title: Why do proximal complement inhibitors require more aggressive vaccination protocols than C5 inhibitors?
answer: Proximal inhibitors block C3-mediated opsonization, causing broader immunosuppression and higher encapsulated bacterial infection risk.
detail: C3 plays a critical role in opsonizing bacteria for phagocytosis, which is impaired by C3, factor B, or factor D inhibitors.
Both C5 and proximal inhibitors require meningococcal ACWY and B vaccines plus continuous antibiotic prophylaxis.
Proximal inhibitors necessitate stronger emphasis on pneumococcal PCV and PPSV23 vaccines plus Haemophilus influenzae type b vaccination.
All vaccines should be completed at least two weeks before initiating any complement inhibitor therapy.
releaseNote: Proximal complement inhibitors require more aggressive vaccination protocols than C5 inhibitors due to broader immunosuppression and increased risk of encapsulated bacterial infections.
